title: PI Permission System Configuration
description: Unified config file combining runtime knobs and permission policy for pi-permission-system.
markdownDescription:
Unified config file combining runtime knobs and permission policy for [pi-permission-system](https://github.com/gotgenes/pi-permission-system).

Place at `~/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-permission-system/config.json` (global) or `<project>/.pi/extensions/pi-permission-system/config.json` (project).

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for editor autocomplete and validation.

debugLog.description: Write verbose permission-system diagnostics to the extension logs directory.
debugLog.markdownDescription: Write verbose permission-system diagnostics to `logs/pi-permission-system-debug.jsonl` under the extension config directory.

permissionReviewLog.description: Write permission request and decision audit events to the extension logs directory.
permissionReviewLog.markdownDescription: Write permission request and decision audit events to `logs/pi-permission-system-permission-review.jsonl` under the extension config directory.

yoloMode.description: Auto-approve ask-state permission checks, including subagent approval forwarding.
yoloMode.markdownDescription:
Auto-approve `ask`-state permission checks, including subagent approval forwarding.

⚠️ **Use with caution** — this disables all interactive confirmation prompts.

defaultPolicy.description: Fallback permission state per category when no specific rule matches.
defaultPolicy.markdownDescription:
Fallback permission state per category when no specific rule matches.

All fields default to `"ask"` when omitted.

tools.description: Default permission for registered tools when no exact-name rule matches in the tools map.
tools.markdownDescription: Default permission for registered tools when no exact-name rule matches in the `tools` map.

bash.description: Default permission for bash commands when no pattern in the bash map matches.
bash.markdownDescription: Default permission for bash commands when no pattern in the `bash` map matches.

mcp.description: Default permission for MCP operations when no target pattern in the mcp map matches.
mcp.markdownDescription: Default permission for MCP operations when no target pattern in the `mcp` map matches.

skills.description: Default permission for skill loading when no pattern in the skills map matches.
skills.markdownDescription: Default permission for skill loading when no pattern in the `skills` map matches.

special.description: Default permission for special checks (external_directory) when no explicit rule matches.
special.markdownDescription: Default permission for special checks (`external_directory`) when no explicit rule matches.

tools.description: Exact-name permissions for registered tools. Use this map for the canonical Pi built-ins and any extension-provided or third-party tools.
tools.markdownDescription:
Exact-name permissions for registered tools. Use this map for the canonical Pi built-ins (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `grep`, `find`, `ls`) and any extension-provided or third-party tools.

No wildcards — keys must match the registered tool name exactly.

bash.description: Wildcard pattern permissions for bash commands. Patterns use * globs matched against the full command string. Last matching rule wins.
bash.markdownDescription:
Wildcard pattern permissions for bash commands. Patterns use `*` globs matched against the full command string.

**Last matching rule wins** — put broad catch-all rules first and specific overrides after them.

mcp.description: Pattern-based permissions for targets invoked through a registered mcp tool when available.
mcp.markdownDescription:
Pattern-based permissions for targets invoked through a registered `mcp` tool when available.

Targets include server names (`myServer`), qualified tool names (`myServer:search`, `myServer_search`), and baseline operations (`mcp_status`, `mcp_list`, `mcp_connect`, `mcp_describe`, `mcp_search`).

skills.description: Wildcard pattern permissions for skill names. Controls which skills can be loaded or read from disk.
skills.markdownDescription:
Wildcard pattern permissions for skill names. Controls which skills can be loaded or read from disk.

Use `"*": "ask"` to require confirmation for all skills, or `"dangerous-*": "deny"` to block a family of skills.

special.description: Reserved permission checks for special runtime behaviors.

external_directory.description: Enforces permission checks for path-bearing file tools (read, write, edit, find, grep, ls) when they target paths outside the active working directory.
external_directory.markdownDescription:
Enforces permission checks for path-bearing file tools (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `find`, `grep`, `ls`) when they target paths outside the active working directory.

Evaluated **before** the normal tool permission check — so `tools.read: "allow"` can permit ordinary reads while `external_directory: "ask"` still requires confirmation for paths outside `cwd`.

tool_call_limit.description: Deprecated and ignored. This key has no effect and should be removed from your config.
tool_call_limit.markdownDescription: ⚠️ **Deprecated and ignored.** This key has no effect and should be removed from your config.

permissionState.description: A permission decision: allow (permit silently), deny (block with error), or ask (prompt the user for confirmation).

oneOf.description: Permit the action silently with no user interaction.
Block the action with an error message. The agent is told not to retry.
Prompt the user for confirmation via the interactive UI before proceeding.

permissionMap.description: A map of name or wildcard patterns to permission states. Keys are matched against the relevant target (tool name, bash command, MCP target, or skill name).
permissionMap.markdownDescription:
A map of name or wildcard patterns to permission states.

Keys are matched against the relevant target. Use `*` for wildcard matching. When multiple patterns match, the **last matching rule wins**.

propertyNames.description: A non-empty pattern string. Use * for wildcard matching.
